title: rubus tereticaulis var. henrici
intro: Rubus tereticaulis var. henrici, also known as the Pacific blackberry, is a variety of thorny shrub native to the Pacific coast of North America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce pain and inflammation, and boost the immune system. The health benefits of Rubus tereticaulis var. henrici include its ability to support cardiovascular health, improve cognitive function, and aid in the treatment of various infections. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used fresh or frozen in jams, preserves, and other culinary applications.
